title: Spermacoce luteovirens
taxonomy: Spermacoce luteovirens is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the Rubiaceae family, which includes a large number of species commonly known as coffee or madder family. This family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, and Spermacoce luteovirens is classified within the tribe Spermacoceae. The Rubiaceae family is a part of the order Gentianales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. The classification of Spermacece luteovirens reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Spermacoce luteovirens is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used in various cultures to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and infections. Some studies suggest that the plant may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and potential applications. Further investigation is required to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use.

Studies have shown that the plant extracts have the ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria, fungi, and viruses, making it a potential candidate for the development of new antimicrobial agents. intro: Spermacoce luteovirens, also known as yellow green Spermacoce or yellow green Spermacoce weed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae. Traditionally, Spermacoce luteovirens has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Spermacoce luteovirens is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, and is commonly used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
